title: pisum arvense var. umbellatum
intro: Pisum arvense var. umbellatum, also known as sweet pea or garden pea, is a leguminous plant species within the Pisum genus, characterized by its climbing habit and fragrant flowers. The medicinal properties of Pisum arvense var. umbellatum include its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic effects, which contribute to its traditional use in treating conditions such as urinary tract infections and inflammation. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, which may help to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Pisum arvense var. umbellatum is often used as a tea, tincture, or dried extract to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also used as an ornamental plant in gardens due to its fragrant flowers and attractive appearance.
